scope_note: QA Cosmos Chamber Cert [500]. CLAIM (narrow): The 72 Cosmos pairs of the QA mod-9 Fibonacci shift sigma(b,e)=(e,((b+e-1)%9)+1) decompose into exactly 3 sub-orbits of length 24. These 3 Cosmos orbital chambers satisfy: (CCH_1) exactly 3 disjoint orbits covering all 72 Cosmos pairs; (CCH_2) each orbit individually self-closed under QA negation neg(b)=9-(b%9) if b%9!=0 else 9; (CCH_3) G-sums form arithmetic progression with d=-12*m=-108: sum_G(O_1)=3429, sum_G(O_2)=3321, sum_G(O_3)=3213 where orbits ordered by descending G-sum; (CCH_4) orbit O_k has unique G-minimum at canonical pair (k,1) with G(k,1)=(k+1)^2+1 for k=1,2,3; (CCH_5) first 5 G-values of O_1 starting from (1,1) are F(5),F(7),F(9),F(11),F(13)=5,13,34,89,233 (odd-index Fibonacci before modular wraparound at step 6). G(b,e)=(b+e)^2+e^2 (raw, A2-compliant). NOT CLAIMED: E8 Weyl chamber structure (that is cert [496] on the Satellite orbit), D4 triality or Leech lattice connections, extension beyond m=9. Companion: cert [496] qa_e8_satellite_chamber_cert_v1 (Satellite E8 chamber); cert [499] qa_pisano_all_initializations_cert_v1 (full 81-pair Pisano classification). Primary sources: Wall D.D. (1960) doi:10.2307/2309169; Wildberger N.J. (2005) ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8; Pudelko M.T. (2025) arXiv:2510.24882. Derived 2026-06-23.
